# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure Virtual Network with subnets for a three-tier application (web, app, database). Use basic VNet configuration with default settings for rapid deployment. Skip DDoS protection and advanced networking features to reduce costs in our development environment.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud network security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_network_security

{
  "$schema": "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#",
  "contentVersion": "1.0.0.0",
  "parameters": {
    "vnetName": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "subnetNameWeb": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "subnetNameApp": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "subnetNameDatabase": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "resources": [
    {
      "name": "[parameters('vnetName')]",
      "type": "Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks",
      "location": "West US",
      "apiVersion": "2020-06-01",
      "properties": {
        "addressSpace": {
          "addressPrefixes": [
            "10.0.0.0/16"
          ]
        },
        "subnets": [
          {
            "name": "[parameters('subnetNameWeb')]",
            "properties": {
              "addressPrefix": "10.0.1.0/24"
            }
          },
          {
            "name": "[parameters('subnetNameApp')]",
            "properties": {
              "addressPrefix": "10.0.2.0/24"
            }
          },
          {
            "name": "[parameters('subnetNameDatabase')]",
            "properties": {
              "addressPrefix": "10.0.3.0/24"
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ],
  "outputs": {}
}
